Midnight NIGHT Token Debug Report

On-chain vs API schedule discrepancy analysis

Key Findings

Analysis of 52,774 eligible addresses, 52,774 API schedules, and 55,731 on-chain transactions reveals a single root cause manifesting as multiple symptoms:

The Midnight backend fails to recognize successful on-chain thaw transactions. When a first-thaw claim succeeds on-chain but the backend marks it as skipped or failed, three things happen:

  1. 616 addresses have thaw #0 marked skipped despite all 616 having a successful on-chain redeem transaction
  2. 616 addresses have thaw #1 amounts inflated because the backend rolled the "unclaimed" amount forward
  3. 4209 thaws show as "redeemable" in the API but the NIGHT was already delivered on-chain — users see a claim button for tokens they already received

These issues are not correlated with shared escrow addresses or dirty funding UTXOs — rates match the baseline population.

StatusCountNotes
skipped616all thaw #0, all have successful on-chain tx
failed56all have tx_id, 56 tx_ids not found on-chain
confirming2stuck in confirming state

Suspicious Status 674

616 thaw #0 marked "skipped" despite successful on-chain tx. 56 marked "failed" (56 with tx_ids not found on-chain). 2 stuck in "confirming".
